01-concurrency: Amdahl's Law: maximum speedup is capped by the serial fraction. Even at 95\% parallel, 256 cores yield only ~17x — the serial 5\% dominates.
Deadlock needs all four Coffman conditions at once; the tell-tale sign is a cycle in the wait-for graph. Break any one condition (e.g. lock ordering) to prevent it.
aiml-01-fund: Total generalization error decomposes into bias$^2$ + variance + irreducible noise. Minimizing it means balancing model capacity, not maximizing fit.
A large train–validation gap that closes with more data signals variance; high error on both that more data won't fix signals bias.
ROC summarizes ranking quality across thresholds; PR is more informative under heavy class imbalance, where a high AUC can still hide poor precision.
Every classifier error is a false positive or false negative. Precision penalizes FPs, recall penalizes FNs — which matters more depends on the cost of each mistake.
aiml-03-dl: Nonlinearities let networks model non-linear functions. ReLU/GELU dominate deep nets (cheap, no saturation for x>0); sigmoid/tanh saturate and can vanish gradients.
Each step moves opposite the gradient. Elongated (ill-conditioned) valleys make vanilla gradient descent zig-zag across the steep axis — motivating momentum, RMSProp and Adam.
A feed-forward net stacks fully-connected layers: each neuron is a weighted sum of the previous layer plus a bias, passed through a nonlinearity.
Convolution + pooling progressively reduce spatial size while increasing channel depth, building features from edges → textures → objects, then a dense head classifies.
dsa-03-algos: Asymptotic class, not constant factors, decides scalability. Beyond modest n, an O(n log n) algorithm crushes O(n²); exponential is infeasible past ~n=30.
No single sort wins everywhere: merge sort is stable and worst-case O(n log n) but needs O(n) space; quicksort is faster in practice but O(n²) worst case.
09-performance: Orders of magnitude separate cache, memory, SSD, disk and network. Designing for performance means counting which tier each operation touches.
A handful of slow requests barely move the mean but dominate user experience. Track p95/p99, not averages — at scale, the tail is what every user eventually hits.
End-to-end latency is a sum over hops. Instrument every hop, find the one dominating the budget, and optimize that — don't guess.
Width = time spent. A wide bar with little above it is a self-time hot spot. Find the widest plateau near the top and optimize that function first.
07-distributed: When the network partitions, a distributed store must sacrifice either consistency (AP) or availability (CP). Partition tolerance is non-negotiable.
Consistency is a dial, not a switch. Stronger models are easier to reason about but cost availability and latency under partitions — pick per use-case.
Keys map to the first node clockwise on a hash ring. Adding/removing a node remaps only ~1/N of keys (not all of them as in naive modulo hashing).
2PC gives atomic commit across nodes: a prepare vote then a commit/abort decision. Its weakness — if the coordinator dies after prepare, participants block holding locks.
03-networks: Each layer adds a header and treats the layer above as opaque payload. Interview framing: name the layer, its addressing unit, and a protocol.
TCP opens with SYN / SYN-ACK / ACK to agree on sequence numbers, then tears down with a four-way FIN/ACK exchange. This reliability is what UDP trades away for speed.
The resolver does the legwork: it walks the hierarchy (root → TLD → authoritative) on the client's behalf, then caches the answer for the TTL.
02-os: FCFS is simple but suffers the convoy effect; Round Robin time-slices for fairness and responsiveness at the cost of extra context switches.
Each level trades capacity for speed by orders of magnitude. Performance comes from keeping the working set in the fastest tier that fits — the basis of caching.
A process sees a flat virtual address space: code and globals at the bottom, the heap growing up and the stack growing down toward each other, kernel mapped on top.
aiml-04-tf: Each query token forms a weighted average over all tokens via softmax(QKᵀ/√d). Rows sum to 1 — the model learns which context positions matter.
A transformer layer = self-attention (mix information across tokens) + a position-wise MLP, each wrapped in a residual connection and LayerNorm. Stack N of them.
04-cloud: Static peak provisioning wastes capacity (red area) most of the day. Autoscaling tracks demand in steps, trading a little headroom for large cost savings.
Moving from IaaS to SaaS hands more of the stack to the provider. Knowing the boundary tells you what you must still secure, patch and scale yourself.
Each layer abstracts the one below: hardware → hypervisor → containers → orchestration → mesh → delivery → observability, all declared as code.
05-system-design: The canonical pattern: stateless app servers behind a load balancer, a CDN for static edge content, a cache to absorb reads, and a primary DB with read replicas.
Latency stays flat until utilization approaches capacity, then rises sharply as queues build. Plan for headroom — running hot (>70-80\%) is a tail-latency trap.
The four write/read cache patterns trade freshness, latency and durability. Cache-aside is the default; write-through favours consistency; write-back favours write latency.
A topic is split into partitions for parallelism. Within a consumer group each partition is read by exactly one consumer; different groups read the same data independently.
06-lld: Strategy encapsulates interchangeable algorithms behind one interface. The Context delegates to a strategy object, so new behaviors are added without modifying it.
08-databases: B+ trees keep data sorted with a high branching factor, so lookups touch only O(log n) nodes (few disk pages). Linked leaves make range queries cheap.
Stronger isolation prevents more anomalies but costs concurrency. Serializable is safest; most OLTP systems default to Read Committed and add locks where needed.
10-security: TLS 1.3 establishes a shared secret via key shares in the first flight, so encrypted application data flows after just one round trip (0-RTT on resumption).
No single control is trusted alone. Independent layers — perimeter, network, host, app, data — mean an attacker must defeat each in turn.
11-behavioral: Structure every behavioral story as Situation → Task → Action → Result. Lead with context, but spend most words on your specific actions and a measurable result.
dsa-01-patterns: Instead of recomputing each window from scratch (O(n·k)), slide the window: add the entering element and drop the leaving one, achieving O(n).
On sorted input, two pointers from both ends turn an O(n²) pair-search into O(n): move the pointer that brings the sum toward the target.
Most coding-interview problems map to a small set of patterns. Read the input shape and the asked-for quantity, and this guide points you to the right tool.
Is array sorted or can we sort it?
dsa-02-ds: Pick the structure that makes your hot operation O(1)/O(log n). Hash tables win for lookups; balanced trees keep order; heaps give cheap min/max.
aiml-05-genai: RAG embeds the query, retrieves the most similar chunks from a vector store, and injects them into the prompt — grounding the LLM in current, citable facts.
Reach for the cheapest fix first: prompting, then few-shot, then RAG for knowledge gaps; fine-tune only when format or domain accuracy still falls short.
aiml-06-mlops: Production ML is a loop: monitoring detects data/concept drift and triggers retraining, closing the gap between a one-off model and a living system.
sdp-1: A bucket refills at a steady rate up to a capacity. Each request spends a token; an empty bucket throttles. This smooths traffic while allowing short bursts.
sdp-2: Sharding partitions data by key to scale writes across nodes; replication copies each shard for read scaling and failover. Most large datastores combine both.
Modeling the ride as an explicit state machine makes valid transitions (and the events that trigger them) unambiguous — the backbone of the matching and trip services.
lld-coded: Observer lets a subject notify many observers of state changes without knowing their concrete types — the basis of event systems and reactive UIs.
12-oop: OOP rests on four pillars (A-PIE). Abstraction and encapsulation manage complexity; inheritance and polymorphism enable reuse and extension.
The UML vocabulary for class relationships, weakest to strongest: dependency, association, aggregation (hollow diamond), composition (filled diamond), inheritance (hollow triangle).
Inheritance hard-wires an is-a hierarchy at compile time; composition assembles behavior from swappable parts. Favor composition — it stays flexible and testable.
SOLID keeps object-oriented code changeable: each class focused (S), extended not edited (O), substitutable (L), with lean interfaces (I) and inverted dependencies (D).
Single Responsibility   one class, one reason to change
A virtual call resolves to the runtime object's method via a vtable (C++/Java) or MRO lookup (Python). This is what lets one line of code drive types written later.
vtable / MRO lookup on the ACTUAL object type
